lines.speaker: Maid
lines.text: Oh! Please hire me, [sir/madam]! I'm very good, well, I'm not bad, and my fee's only 1000 coins.

lines.speaker: Maid
lines.text: Well, I can, um. I can cook meals and make tea and everything, and I can even take things to and from the bank for you. I won't make any mistakes this time and everything will be fine!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about your previous jobs.

lines.speaker: Maid
lines.text: Oh! Oh! I, well, I, er. It wasn't really my fault, I mean, it was, but not really. I mean, how was I to know that that plate was so valuable? It was just lying around and I don't know art, it just looked like a pretty pattern and

lines.speaker: Maid
lines.text: I just had to use it because there weren't enough plates. And no one had told me that Dennis was off sick and hadn't fed the dogs so I wasn't expecting them to jump up at me when I was carrying the food and it, it

lines.speaker: Maid
lines.text: went all over the place, gravy and potatoes and bits of fine porcelain and I'm so sorry. It won't happen again, I promise.

lines.speaker: Maid
lines.text: Oh! Oh dear! I'm terribly sorry, [sir/madam], but I'm not allowed to work for anyone unless they have level 25 Construction. If a house is poorly made I might damage it by mistake!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're hired... well, I don't have the money on me, but can I owe you? I'm good for it, honest.

lines.speaker: Maid
lines.text: Oh... I'm terribly sorry, [sir/madam], but I'd really prefer to be paid before I start work.

lines.speaker: Maid
lines.text: Oh! Oh, thank you [sir/madam], thank you!
